title: Turn owner corrections into bounded feedback
description: Record attributable acceptance, rejection, and correction feedback against exact outcomes and propose only explicit narrow rule changes. Implements G4 and UC-CLASSIFY-005.
rationale: Corrections should improve future assistance without turning a one-off exception into automatic generalized behavior.
story_want: use an accepted, rejected, or corrected outcome as attributable feedback
story_so_that: future rule proposals improve without silently changing rules or Ledger history
acceptance_criteria: Given an accepted or rejected outcome, when feedback is recorded, then it references the exact transaction, evaluation, outcome, normalization, rule-set, and contributing rule versions plus owner actor, reason, and timestamp.
Given a category correction completed in LEDGER, when CLASSIFY records feedback, then it references the authoritative prior and resulting Ledger allocations without rewriting either.
Given one correction, when CLASSIFY derives a response, then it records no proposal or one smallest-scope retire, narrow, or replacement proposal.
Given a feedback-derived proposal, when it is recorded, then CLASSIFY does not activate the proposal automatically.
Given a feedback-derived proposal, when it is recorded, then CLASSIFY does not broaden an existing rule automatically.
Given a feedback-derived candidate, when activation is requested, then the same private validation and explicit activation requirements as any other candidate apply.
Given historical feedback, when a rule is retired or superseded, then the feedback remains attributable to the original rule version and outcome.
Given unavailable prior Match Evidence, when feedback is recorded, then CLASSIFY stores the owner decision but creates no rule proposal from reconstructed current evidence.
security_criteria: Feedback results expose only explicit owner-requested provenance and keep private description and normalized-token payloads owner-only.
